prompts.text: Explain MECE to a client analyst joining the working session tomorrow.
Explain to your exec sponsor what a hypothesis tree actually does for the project.
Explain the pyramid principle to the client team before Thursday's readout.
Explain to the CIO what 'so what' means in our recommendation slides.
Explain to the change lead what a Day-One plan covers in plain language.
Explain to a procurement lead what hours-based vs fixed-fee billing actually means.
Explain the firm's three-week diagnostic to a CEO who's never bought consulting before.
Explain to a skeptical CFO what 'hypothesis-driven' means without sounding evasive.
Explain to your new associate what an issue tree is and why we build one first.
Explain to a hostile steering committee what a no-regrets move is.
Did the diagnostic prove the cost-out hypothesis?
Should the client launch in Q3 or delay to Q4?
Is the operating-model recommendation still the right call after the new data?
Was Tuesday's working session a productive one?
Should we extend the engagement another six weeks?
Did the pilot site hit the savings target?
Is the CIO's pushback going to kill the IT consolidation track?
Should the steering committee approve the new go-live date?
Was the partner right to challenge our cost baseline?
Should we walk away from the scope creep on workstream three?
Explain to the CFO how the hypothesis tree narrows the savings opportunity.
Explain to the client team what waterfalling capacity from old to new processes feels like.
Explain to a junior client how an executive summary differs from a status update.
Explain to the steering committee why phased rollouts beat big-bang launches here.

Explain to the COO how change-management investment compounds over the rollout.
Explain to the procurement lead why a fixed-fee model carries risk back to us.
Explain to the board how our run-rate savings model gets to the headline number.
Explain to a client SME what 'driver-based forecasting' really means for their P&L.
Explain to a hostile IT director how our migration de-risks itself wave by wave.
Explain 'zero-based budgeting' to a frontline manager who's never seen consulting before.
Explain the firm's 'value-capture roadmap' to a plant manager on the floor.
Explain 'target operating model' to the HR business partner across the table.
Explain 'span-of-control benchmarks' to a director whose org you're about to flatten.
Explain 'synergy realization' to a CFO who's heard it from three firms already.
Explain 'workstream' to a client analyst on day one of the engagement.
Explain 'EBITDA bridge' to a non-finance executive in the steering committee.
Explain 'change readiness' to a union rep before the workforce announcement.
Explain 'pace layering' to a CIO who's seen too many consulting frameworks.
Explain 'agile cadence' to a finance director who runs everything in waterfall.
Why is the $40M savings number on page 7 defensible to a skeptical CFO?
Why does Thursday's steering committee need to approve the org redesign now, not next month?
Why is our recommendation different from the last three firms who tried this?
Why does the IT director's objection about timeline actually make our case stronger?
Why should the client trust your interim recommendation when the analysis isn't done?
Why is the pilot site the right place to start, not the largest region?
Why does the change lead need a dedicated budget line, not just a workstream?
Why should the exec sponsor fund a six-week extension after a missed milestone?
Why is your hypothesis still right after the data pulled in pointed the other way?
Why should a board override the COO's pushback on the workforce plan?
What's the single biggest risk to landing this engagement on time?
What's the one reason the client should approve the org redesign?
Why did Tuesday's working session miss its decision target?
What's the single biggest source of value in the cost-out program?
What's the one finding from the diagnostic the partner most needs to hear?
Why is the change lead the only person who can unblock the rollout?
What's the one number that defines whether the pilot worked?
Why is the IT director the right escalation path for the data issue?
What's the single biggest reason the client's last transformation failed?
Why is now the right moment to recommend a strategic pause?
Explain the difference between a recommendation and a decision.
Explain the difference between a working session and a steering committee.
Explain the difference between a diagnostic and an implementation phase.
Explain the difference between gross savings and net realized savings to the CFO.
Explain the difference between an executive summary and a chapter cover slide.
Explain the difference between scope creep and a legitimate change request.
Explain the difference between an MD-led readout and a partner check-in.
Explain the difference between change resistance and capacity issues to the sponsor.
Explain why a verbal nod from the sponsor isn't an approved decision.
Explain the difference between an interim recommendation and a final answer.
Tell me why the $80M cost-out target is achievable in 18 months.
Tell me why the operating-model recommendation beats the status quo.
Tell me why this engagement is on track despite missing last week's milestone.
Tell me why the pilot region's results will scale to the full footprint.
Tell me why the client's IT team can absorb this migration on the proposed timeline.
Tell me why your three-week diagnostic deserves another six weeks of work.
Tell me why your change-management plan won't repeat the last firm's failure.
Tell me why the steering committee should reject the CIO's counter-proposal.
Tell me why our recommendation is right even though the client SMEs disagree.
Tell me why your associate's slide is actually the cleanest in the deck.
Pitch the engagement's central finding to the CEO in one breath.
Summarize the workstream's status for the partner in the hallway.
Describe the engagement's ideal client outcome in one sentence.
Explain why Thursday's steering committee got pushed a week.

Summarize the client's biggest objection to the operating model.
Describe your champion on the client side in one sentence.
Brief the MD on the engagement at risk of slipping the final readout.

Convince the client's CFO that procurement should sign off this week.
Argue why the engagement deserves a six-week extension at full burn rate.

Tell the story of an engagement that almost collapsed in week three.
Walk me through the toughest partner review of your career.
Tell the story of a skeptical client executive who gradually became your strongest supporter.
Walk me through a steering committee that voted against your recommendation.

Recount a deliverable that landed flat in front of the CEO.

Tell the story of a change initiative that worked despite real resistance.
Walk me through the toughest client relationship you've had, and how it ended.
Recount a partner who challenged your hypothesis ten minutes before the readout.
Should we extend the engagement another six weeks or hand off now?
Pilot first or full rollout in the largest region?
Is the CIO's pushback a real blocker or a stall tactic?
Escalate to the exec sponsor or work it through the client team?
Take the smaller fixed-fee phase or push for the full transformation mandate?
Should we walk from the scope creep or absorb it for the client relationship?

Recommend the operating model the CFO will accept or the one the data supports?
Replace the underperforming associate or coach them through the next milestone?
Hold the line on the recommendation or soften it to keep the client engaged?
Three of the client's SMEs missed the last two working sessions. Address it.
The client team can't produce the data we need by Friday. Solve it.
The change lead just told you they don't have headcount for the rollout. Address it.
Your associate is rebuilding the same slide for the third time. Solve it.

The IT director keeps reopening decisions the steering committee already approved. Solve it.

Workstream two has missed its second milestone in a row. Talk about it.
Two of your top client champions just got reorged out. Address it.
The board just asked why month-three savings are at 20% of plan. Respond.

Centralized vs federated operating model for the client's new function.

Pivoting the recommendation to match the CFO vs holding the data-backed answer.
